channel.title: SCP Foundation - new forum posts
channel.description: Posts in forums of the site "SCP Foundation" - Secure, Contain, Protect

item.title: Re: (Seeking greenlights and tips) An army of darkness
item.content:encoded:
Hello Dr Itzhak! let's take a look :)

SAND

+ show block
– hide block

The concept:

Elevator Pitch: In Antarctica a dark portal to another dimension suddenly appears, after some scientists get lost in Antarctica the foundation started an investigation. That is when they found the dark warriors. Lead by a general corrupted by darkness.

Overall, this concept feels a bit un-engaging to me. As it stands there isn't really anything that separates this/makes it stand out from similar ideas (theres a few 'dark armies' that exist already in the media). Just having a 'dark army', with no further context won't really be able to carry the article, as it doesn't offer much to get invested in, or much to look into behind, "an evil army of evil dark things will invade us, but we don't know why".

What exactly are they're motives?- currently their isn't really a reason for them to doing this, which makes the narrative feel a bit flat, as there doesn't seem to be any reason for the end goal. what actually is this "darkness"? -what role will it play in the narrative? what about the skip will make me as the reader invested?

The narrative:

Central Narrative: After the foundation finds the portal and the creatures a provisional site is built around the portal. The SCP includes two entities, the general and his soldiers (proxies). The general commands his proxies or the proxies act on their own attacking any life-forms not "Embracing the dark". The general and his proxies are dark they don't have: skin, skeletons or even organs. the general is rarely seen his soldiers are mostly in our dimension and not in the "Dark" dimension. Both the soldiers and the general can corrupt biological life forms by killing them, when a human is killed a dark goo consumes them and turns them into a soldier but if the body is too damaged or did not die by the hands of the entities no transformation will happen. All dark entities have a severe case of Photophobia1 since light can hurt and melt them. Inside the portal there seems to be a world which had a SK-Class Dominance Shift Scenario where the entities took over earth in that dimension but humans still survived and are near extinction. The foundation also found the general is building an army which could result in a BI Class Pre-Invasion Scenario.

As for the narrative, This starts off fine, but then seems to turn into additional info on the skip? What events take place during the narrative? how was the skip found initially? who are the characters? how are the questions raised in the concept answered? whats the payoff?- what is the narrative building up to?

Hook/Attention-Grabber: The attention grabber here is the army. It's goals are yet unknown and it's size is large and feared. I think readers would want to continue reading to know the mysterious outcome of the article.

Do note that the skip doesn't need to be dangerous to be interesting, what about the skips themselves are interesting? why should I as the reader care about the concept (or what takes place in the narrative?).

Red or green?
Due to my afore mentioned concerns, no-greenlight.

If you would like me review this (or any concept) further, then please feel free to send me a PM or ask via IRC. Response times generally range from 2-12 hours over PMs. If your looking for additional critiques/perspectives in general, then I would highly recommend contacting the butterfly squad :)

Cheers,

RED


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: (Seeking greenlights and tips) An army of darkness

item.title: Also known as: The Mekhanics Take Flight!
item.content:encoded:
Ahhh this has been a bit stressful writing it to make sure I didn't downgrade the characters or story! AM sorry for the wait!

Anyways on to the thanks! I would like to thank Cydhra, Mooagain, Vivarium, Mars, and Quadraginta for the critique and willingness to help me make sure this is as good as I can do with my… lack of writing techniques. I also wanna thank MalyceGraves and plaidypus for creating both the world of La Rue Macabre and the Mekhanics cause it is a lot of fun to write for the world.

And as always thank you for reading this, reader! I'm just happy to know your reading this.

(PS I am very sorry for the stiff prose, my skills are still terrible at writing in tale form)


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: The Mekhanics of La Rue Part Two

item.title: Re: Mind blown
item.content:encoded:
Don’t go around giving Harry ideas :P


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: The Moloch Mentality

item.title: Re: Seeing page comment replies
item.content:encoded:
They just check back to the replies of places they've commented.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Questions Desk
Forum thread: Seeing page comment replies

I appreciate the dialogue between the O5 and the CSR guy, but I think this article could have been a lot more. Maybe sending the O5 further and further up the Customer Service chain would have been the cincher for me, but I'm not sure. The article is written well enough it's just missing something.


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5197

item.title: (Seeking green lights) Ever wonder where your things went?
item.content:encoded:
Seeking green lights: yes

Page type: SCP article

Class: Euclid

Elevator pitch: This SCP is a female elf who has the ability to summon items from anywhere in the multi verse. However even though it sounds like X-men syndrome, she can only summon items that she has a complete understanding of how they are made and/or works.
For example she knows how a T-shirt is made so she can summon one, and she knows how a lightbulb is made and works so she can make one. She has a photographic memory that helps her summon objects. The objects come through a multi-universal portal that appears and disappears within the space of 0.2 seconds.
She has a standard - simple humanoid containment cell and has been allowed access to level 01 areas of the facility she is housed in. She is very cooperative with foundation staff, seemingly understanding of why she is locked up. However she has been declared as a threat because the staff cannot say if she is lying or not. Multiple researchers have agreed that she must be monitored and her cell is to be fully checked every two days as well as all actions done by this SCP. The foundation agrees that she is a dangerous scp and should be heavily monitored

Hook/Attention-Grabber: She can summon life forms that she knows the biology of, however no summond-life form has made it out of the portal in one piece, sometimes it may be missing a limb or patch of fur, or it may just be a bloody mess.

She was discovered 1 year ago when foundation staff noticed a sudden surge in gamma radiation in the area. After having onsite personnel in the area for a day, a portal opened up reveling the SCP and an unknown entity behind it. The unknown entity pushed SCP through and closed the portal behind her. She then quickly surrend willingly.
She came from a universe where her race was being hunted and killed. Her race was able to summon items and track each other via a form of telepathy. The limits of this are unknown but the SCP claims that her race frequently traveled the multi verse aided by a machine they used. Researchers have agreed that a small group of mtf should be close by and ready to terminate the SCP if she shows any signs of hostility.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: (Seeking green lights) Ever wonder where your things went?

item.title: Mind blown
item.content:encoded:
If the SCP brand ever hits the "big screen", this tale is movie material. +1


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: The Moloch Mentality

item.title: Re: The Laughing Tree
item.content:encoded:
Heyo followup!

SAND

+ show block
– hide block


How will you plan to show all of this in the narrative? currently there is only really interviews, but for some of this information it sounds like there would need to be some sort investigation or further tests.- The child wouldn't be able to show much outside of their own experiences.

Regarding your point about how the tree is called to the Foundation's attention. It's true that this is a little lacking. But I think that the foundation would be interested in rumors/tales that allude to a possible SCP entity. Perhaps the investigation department checks the background for names written on the tree and finds a pattern of anti-social behavior among them. Is there something in SCP that could possible help with this point? like a signature for a nefarious group or individual?

Hmm… I'm still kind of skeptical on this, the Foundation isn't likely (at least not to me), to take a scout tale of all things seriously enough to look further into it. Unless there is some sort evidence to show otherwise, I don't think the Foundation being curious will work.-do note that even if they did look at the tree/camp ground, they're likely not going to see anything immediately off. They would really need to know what they're looking for if they wanted something concrete.

-Perhaps you could work off this subtly a little bit? with it taking a while for the child to convince their parents and furthermore the Foundation that this is something more than a particularly bad camp experience?

That said;

like a signature for a nefarious group or individual?

You could go this route if you want to, however you would need to prepared to expand a bit more on the individual responsible for this. Along with showing a bit more on why the tree was created.

Due to my afore mentioned concerns, no-greenlight

If you would like me review this (or any concept) further, then please feel free to send me a PM or ask via IRC. Response times generally range from 2-12 hours over PMs. If your looking for additional critiques/perspectives in general, then I would highly recommend contacting the butterfly squad :)

Cheers,

RED


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: The Laughing Tree

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hello there,
The SCP foundation first caught my attention when i saw a video about it on yt. Ive been reading through the SCP files a bit, and i finally decided i wanna join and create my own SCPs. I am already flowing over with ideas, and have already started working on a file.

Personally i think kebab is best food, as its is made fast, and if made good, is the perfekt balance between meat, salad and bread.

Anyways, im looking forward to being part of this community


Forum category: Site Announcements and Proposals / Introductions
Forum thread: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers

I likensome parts of this article a lot, but there are things I really didn't understand, and one of my pet peeves is authors who leave unexplained mysteries that they purposefully don't explain in the comments. No vote.


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: Dr. Michaels

item.title: Containment question
item.content:encoded:
So Im writing an SCP that has base requirements of supplies (food) to survive… would that information go in the containment section of the article?


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Questions Desk
Forum thread: Containment question

item.title: JS Staff Post -- Forum Crit -- Info
item.content:encoded:
Author,

Per the required reading forum guidelines, please fill out the concept template to seek feedback on your idea:

 **Seeking Greenlights:** Put yes or no. **Page Type:** SCP Article, Tale, GoI Format, Joke SCP, Site Dossier, Other (specify) **Genre (Optional):** Horror, Drama/Emotional, Comedy, Action, History, Other (specify) **Page Layout (Optional):** [How will you will structure the page in terms of information presentation, or plot development? If you plan on using a unique format/format screw, describe that here.] **Elevator Pitch:** [What is the basic core idea? Use a bullet point list if necessary.] **Central Narrative:** [Describe the story beats or overarching context/"big picture" for your piece. If there is no story content, put "none".] **Hook/Attention-Grabber:** [What is novel/unique about your premise? What is special about your idea here that will make it stand out among the other works already on the mainsite?] **Additional Notes:** [Note any concerns or particular areas of assistance you would like reviewers to focus on.]

Rather than posting a new comment, edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of your first comment.

Remember that the combined text for your answers to the last four sections (Elevator Pitch, Central Narrative, Hook/Attention-Grabber, and Additional Notes) MAY NOT exceed 400 words. For convenience, authors can use this page to check wordcount: https://wordcounter.net/

Once you have revised your post accordingly, please reach out to reviewers to request that they look at your thread. You can find example lists of contacts in the Required Reading thread linked above.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Guitar Jail

item.title: JS Staff Post -- Forum Crit -- Info
item.content:encoded:
Noting that this user already has a thread for this idea here: http://scp-wiki.wikidot.com/forum/t-13973627/guitar-jail#post-4866212

Author, as stated in the Required Reading, do not create a new thread for a concept already on the forum, unless the thread has not been posted on in 14 days or longer. If you wish to make changes to your concept, please edit the original thread instead of creating a new one.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Guitar Jail (Seeking Greenlight)

item.title: What about a Canadien Site?
item.content:encoded:
Hey! So I have seen that there's a Site for (almost) all languages, and I wonder if a country such as Canada or the United Kingdom could have their own Site or a type of SCP/Goi/Canon for them. I know that the other Sites are basically just translation, but it could make people from America wanna see Goi Canadien and England, and after that, they could be curious to see other translations of Sites such as Japan, China, France, German, Russian, Korean Ect.


Forum category: Site Announcements and Proposals / Proposals And Policy
Forum thread: What about a Canadien Site?

item.title: Re: {Seeking Review for Greenlight} The Pawn
item.content:encoded:
Thanks again, I've cut more extraneous features previously presented in the concept to focus more on describing the emotional narrative. The entity's electronic-jamming and schizophrenic properties can be explored more in the drafting stage. I also removed any direct mention of other SCP's to mitigate potential crosslink complications.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: {Seeking Review for Greenlight} The Pawn

Definitely nice to read and I always enjoy some unknown group making contact with earth but I wish there were more details in the page pertaining to the SCP. I understand though the limited knowledge of the foundation which would cause this lack of more detailed info. Anyway really great not too long SCP. +1


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5197

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
I've been sitting on this draft for months now, so I think it's time I put this up.

Thanks to Marcelles_Raynes and Sirslash47 for crit.


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5197

under 000-000-000?
"Requests: A wristwatch which makes an audible "tick" as the second hand moves. Both were approved."

I was wondering about that… someone should probably fix it right? As I understand it, that's just a simple grammar type error, but I'm new

item.title: Re: Want to earn Greenlighting Privileges and/or choose next month's front-page features?
item.content:encoded:
I'm interested in either flight
I mostly want to help giving better critique for people's ideas or articles that haven't been critiqued yet because I have trouble putting what I want to say when I try to critique.
I am looking to learn new skills to help newer and older writers.
I mostly struggle with word choices and grammar.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Want to earn Greenlighting Privileges and/or choose next month's front-page features?

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hello everyone, I am Dr. Nightowl

I have been reading SCPs and watching the videos for a few months now, and think it would fun and interesting to write an SCP myself. I'm not that great of a writer so I am gonna try putting my best foot forward and write some SCPs


Forum category: Site Announcements and Proposals / Introductions
Forum thread: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers

I had to downvote because of the rampant grammar mistakes. However, a cheese grater that can grate literally anything does have potential (it could certainly be turned into an AO, at least) and could lead to some interesting cross-tests. Put this through SPaG checks and flesh out the idea a bit more, and you could have something special.


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5978

item.title: Re: The Perfect Sleeper
item.content:encoded:
There's is an extremely rough draft reddit posting to a monkey paw wish that I'm using as I prepare the process if you'd like it.

Details in it are changing is the only reason I didn't use it for questions.
If you feel like seeing those drafts you can check up on user HornyMephistopheles if you'd like.

Also the forum guidelines and how-to guide asked repeatedly for things to be kept brief.
Not trying to be dismissive, just not going to have multiple pages of add-ons and descriptions here when it was specified to please not do that.
I will happily clarify the things that appropriate posters need clarified or detailed.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: The Perfect Sleeper

item.title: Re: Forum Criticism Team - Captain Post
item.content:encoded:
Got it- thanks so much! I'll read over everything. Probably change my account name, too, haha. It's not at all my actual name it was just the first thing that came to mind since she's a character I already had a name for.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Doctor Blackburn Tale [Seeking Greenlight]

item.title: Staff Post- Deletion Vote
item.content:encoded:
Beginning deletion vote at -11.

If you are not the author and you want to rewrite this article, you may reply to this post asking for the opportunity to do so. Please obtain permission from the author (or the Rewrite Team if this article is older than 6 months) and make sure you copy the page source to your sandbox. Please do not reply to this post for any other reason unless you are staff.


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5127

Hey, if you find a good cop in this world, you take 'em.


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5266

item.title: Guitar Jail
item.content:encoded:
A caucasian man in his late 20's/ early 30's, in what appears to be a stone jail cell with no windows, trapped inside of a guitar.

The man wears an orange prisoner outfit and has no recollection of who he is or how he ended up there. He appears to be missing the thumb, index finger and pinky on his right hand. The man doesn't appear to require any sustenance and cannot reach out of the guitar due to an invisible barrier, although objects can pass through this barrier.

The jail cell appears to be made out of stone and concrete and can be observed through the soundhole of the guitar. There is a light bulb hanging from the centre of the room that keeps the room lit at all times and a bed attached to the wall. Any attempts to damage the cell from outside of the guitar have all failed and resulted in the man feeling great pain throughout his body.

The guitar is a typical 6 string acoustic guitar. The guitar seems to be indestructible. There is a logo and name of the non-existent brand "Slaves to the Sound" on the back of the guitar. Any movements the guitar makes do not appear to effect the jail cell or man. When playing the guitar, the man instinctively and perfectly mimics the playing of the guitar. The sounds produced by the guitar do not possess any anomalous properties.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Guitar Jail (Seeking Greenlight)

A masterpiece. Nothing else, nothing more.

+1


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5057 Additional Documentation

item.title: Guitar Jail
item.content:encoded:
A caucasian man in his late 20's/ early 30's, in what appears to be a stone jail cell with no windows, trapped inside of a guitar.

The man wears an orange prisoner outfit and has no recollection of who he is or how he ended up there. He appears to be missing the thumb, index finger and pinky on his right hand. The man doesn't appear to require any sustenance and cannot reach out of the guitar due to an invisible barrier, although objects can pass through this barrier.

The jail cell appears to be made out of stone and concrete and can be observed through the soundhole of the guitar. There is a light bulb hanging from the centre of the room that keeps the room lit at all times and a bed attached to the wall. Any attempts to damage the cell from outside of the guitar have all failed and resulted in the man feeling great pain throughout his body.

The guitar is a typical 6 string acoustic guitar. The guitar seems to be indestructible. There is a logo and name of the non-existent brand "Slaves to the Sound" on the back of the guitar. Any movements the guitar makes do not appear to effect the jail cell or man. When playing the guitar, the man instinctively and perfectly mimics the playing of the guitar. The sounds produced by the guitar do not possess any anomalous properties.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Guitar Jail

item.title: Re: The Perfect Sleeper
item.content:encoded:
Hello Tarastie,
Echoing the words of fluffal above, I am not an official reviewer either. That said, while you seem somewhat dismissive of the feedback of non-reviewers, so I will be blunt; the concept and narrative needs to be more fleshed out. Obviously I cannot speak on their behalf, but I think that most reviewers will have a difficult time giving out a coveted greenlight without knowing more details.

All that out of the way, I do think you have a good idea at it's core and I think can see the direction you are going. So here are some questions and comments on your concept to hopefully get the ball rolling.

Let's dive in
Let's un-dive in

1. How "human" is the entity. I know you alluded to them having super-strenght and maybe even being immortal possibly, but aside from that; what is their character. Is the aware of his anomaly. I think most people if they discovered that they went into a rampage if they didnt get to bed in time, they'd take extra precautions to make sure it doesn't happen.
2. If I understand correctly; the bed in question can change? That does make containment more difficult, but at the moment it seems exactly that, keter-fication without a reason. You seem to have a narrative you are weaving in regards to this but you don't want to give away the surprise; which I understand. However, I think you need to provide at least a little more detail into the facet of his anomaly to get others on board.
3. As stated prior, the details on the narrative itself seem to either not be fleshed out enough or you are withholding them. As such, it is difficult to give a proper review of this. That said, I think the background of this character could provide some interesting narrative and at the moment is mostly being chalked up to "a wish". Now depending on if you can justify your current narrative, this could be a moot point; but it is something that I think could be expanded on.

That is my review for now. I think you have something here; it just needs to be put on paper more.
- VoxStulti


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: The Perfect Sleeper

item.title: Re: suadeant (seeking greenlights)
item.content:encoded:
Placeholder

Heyo, I'm here. Let's take a look, shall we?

+Let's do it!
-Let's do it!

The Elevator Pitch

I find the Elevator Pitch to be too bland and not fleshed out enough. You're playing with the fears of something lurking in the dark—which is a primal human fear that we've felt since it's literally necessary for our survival—as well as added anomalous voices coming from the dark, which may or may not be telepathic.

Essentially, your anomaly boils down to, 'a mysterious hunter in the dark that works best when either ignored or never noticed.'

The 'never noticed' bit kinda falls flat when it starts…trying to talk to people.

The Central Narrative

It needs to be visited each day and the subject must remain with the entity long enough to confirm that it is in fact still contained.

Some very Series 1-esque 'obey it and bow down to its whim or suffer' vibes here.

The reasoning is to give the entity a large amount of non-dangerous things to tempt the subject with and avoid another D-508905 incident.

First, tell us what the D-508905 incident is, if it's even relevant.

Second, so, it doesn't even care if the people do dangerous things, or if they just feel creeped out?

That's not very threatening.

"could be responsible for some of the most brutal dictators and serial killers who claim to be hearing voices or enacting god's will"

"something that thrives by convincing it's host that it does not exist."

I could see this going places, but the overall concept at the moment falls much too flat at what it's trying to achieve.

It's a creepy thing in the dark that goes bump in the night. What is it? Creepy. What does it do? Makes you act out of character. Too underdeveloped.

My thought is that the entity gets some form of nourishment by it's host acting counter to their normal behavior

Well, expand on that.

Apologies if this was harsh, but so far, this feels like an underdeveloped creepypasta that was developed during some 'shower thoughts' in the afternoon.

Every bit of it needs expansion. The only bit I really, really liked was this:

if you stare into a dark room long enough, you will eventually see something

^^
Try to capture that feeling of creepiness and fear we all instinctively know and work it into an SCP.

Remember, all SCPs nowadays need a story of some kind.

Considering you thought about this in the first place, I'm going to assume you had some weird, freaky experience in the dark? I know I did. I'm sure we all did.

Draw some inspiration from that. Think of some people that this anomaly tormented and write a story around that.

Simply sticking something in a cell and giving us basic D-Class containment logs and attempts doesn't really work anymore.

Anywho, I hope I helped and I bid you a lovely day o7


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: suadeant (seeking greenlights)

item.title: Re: The Perfect Sleeper
item.content:encoded:
No, not like scp-3042.

He's not seeking his bed, rather a sleeping arrangement suitable to a list of conditions uncovered by accident an experiment.

To further clarify, apprehension is due to multiple reports from multiple levels of prisons and detention centers of a man who serving time for repeated breaking and entering who on random occasions simply walks out of his cell into other cells or even out of the prison entirely with the doors opening as if they were not even locked.

That's as much detail as I can give at this time.
Will wait and see if there are questions from users that can help greenlight.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: The Perfect Sleeper

item.title: Seeking Greenlight: Cloning a Thaumiel Human?
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch:

+ show block
– hide block

What will happen when the foundation replace the uses of D-Class by cloning a Thaumiel human SCP? The universe runs by anomalies will eventually correct itself, preventing the core narratives/canon of the universe to be swayed/changed. This SCP describes both the phenomenal surrounding the clones of the Thaumiel human (discussion/interview with related personnel, testing results and changes in the project) and the Thaumiel human himself (concerning his discovery, ability and relationship with the foundation and why the foundation decided to use him)

Central Narrative:

+ show block
– hide block

The document for this Thaumiel class scp will be locked.

A message from the Administrator, detailing the dissolving of the D-Class recruitment department, saying that the O5 is now in charge of D-Class through project-Kxxxxx .

The story will be presented first-hand through lists of testing logs with bunches of D-class (with similar id)which all ended with failure and breaches. With a message from O5-1 to other O5 debating to change the "execution of the project".

Then the story focus on a lead containment specialist (later reveal to be the clone of the thaumiel guy) who sent a message (around a month later) to the O5, saying that every SCPs he worked on has become more aggressive/harder to contain, with a few attachments regarding many SCPs. His message is, of course, denied.

The Doctor creates an SCP draft, describing a phenomenon in which SCPs are now becoming harder to contain etc. The draft was denied.

This is followed by a journal from him, saying that he think the O5 must have been using some anomalies to their own personal gains, creating shifts in reality(which might be the reason why SCPs are getting more dangerous etc.). His evident is supported
by the fact that all his concern/messages are denied. He wants to expose them.

A database for project "kamino" (a reference to the Clone from Star-Wars) locked by level 5 has an unauthorized access by this doctor.

He is reportedly terminated by a mematic agent deployed.

A message from O5-1 to the O5 + D-Class recruitments department, saying that the project must be stopped, judging from their observation on the clones and their effect on the SCPs. Even worse, the doctor journal has been posted, causing mass distrust amongst personnel towards the O5.

Then finally a message from O5 to all personnel, explaining everything concerning the journal, the failed containment of many SCPs and finally publishing the document for the Thaumiel SCP.

The man (original thaumiel person), is a reality-bending man of low magnitude. His presence "calms" organic beings around a small radius. His ability is what made the O5 interested and wish to clone him to help contain other SCPs. The result is the opposite of what they hope for.


Hook/Attention-Grabber:
+ show block
– hide block

Most of the story would make the reader think that the SCP is a phenomenon or reality bending entity effecting "the containment of SCPs", judging from the failed testing logs and reports of more frequent breach in containment. While the document hinted that all the D-Class are "the same person", people might not have spotted it until it is revealed at the end with the documentation for the Thaumiel SCP, his identity, and his involvment with the "clone" project as the original body.


Additional Notes:
Any fix/addition/critique in the story is welcomed. The story might be a little bit long but I tried to shortend it as much as I could . I'll message all of you after any major changes so that you can view and critique them again.
Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Seeking Greenlight: Cloning a Thaumiel Human?

holy shit


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: Petition To Ban Margssentif

item.title: Re: The Perfect Sleeper
item.content:encoded:
What stops them from just keeping him locked in a room with a bed? also this sounds awfully similar to SCP-3042 but instead of a dog it's a guy ,and instead of a person, it's a bed.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: The Perfect Sleeper

If you aren't incredibly busy, I wouldn't mind your thoughts regarding the updated article.
Thank you!


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5127

item.title: Re: The Perfect Sleeper
item.content:encoded:
As above in the details,
It's an individual affected by a wish.
There's a set sleeping time.
For further clarity,

They're really just trying to contain him as his appearances can be alarming, disruptive, and or damaging to surroundings.

Much of the testing is simply trying to figure out exactly how to keep him contained without knowing the length he will go to until they try something different.

Several seemingly harmless changes result in containment breech and recovery.

Also they would most likely see an individual who could possibly be manipulated into going someplace specified and being unstoppable in getting there as useful and test for a way to control that ability.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: The Perfect Sleeper

item.title: Re: Data not redacted? (Seeking Greenlight)
item.content:encoded:
I see, thank you for all critique.

I am going to change all concept while maintaining the idea of "E.C vs O5 Council." with a different idea thread.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Data not redacted? (Seeking Greenlight)

item.title: Re: The Perfect Sleeper
item.content:encoded:
I am not an official reviewer. So:
A. I cannot greenlight unfortunately
B. Take my feedback with a grain of salt compared to that of real reviewers

+ show block
– hide block

I like the idea of a man that just wants to sleep as much as he can(wish I was this SCP), but the Elevator pitch doesn't sit well with me when you said, affected individual is it some kind of thing that targets people to have this kind of effect or is it one person that has this kind?

If the SCP foundation knows it will use its superhuman strength to go to bed why would they try to test it?
How sleepy does the guy have to be for him to turn into the phase that he will immediately try to go to bed?
i don't really know what else to say its a fairly simple idea ,but It would be pretty cool if they have a interview with him maybe he talks more about the wish.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: The Perfect Sleeper

item.title: Re: Chearleader Pom Poms[Seeking Greenlight]
item.content:encoded:
That is true. Forgot about that part of the effect. Well I think that clears up that part of my commentary. Consider this a NON-OFFICIAL Greenlight. I think you have a good idea here and with a little ironing out of the narrative details addressed by the others, I think you could have a great idea. Hopefully i'll get a chance to read it one day.

- Vox


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Chearleader Pom Poms[Seeking Greenlight]

item.title: Re: Reality Benders, Hoppers, and Dimension crossing.
item.content:encoded:
A device that reaches into the either the brains or the entire body of the reality bender, stabling them to the machine so anywhere the reality bender goes, the machine goes also?


Forum category: SCP Universe / Foundation Universe
Forum thread: Reality Benders, Hoppers, and Dimension crossing.

item.title: [Seeking Tips and Possibly, Greenlight] an old arcade console
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch:
An old Arcade Gaming Console, entitled "A Devil's Adventure" which seems to make anyone who play it slowly changes behavior until he/she becomes violent.
It is also noted that after being played, the console just turns off for the rest of the day, turning back on the next day.

Central Narrative:
Discovered in an old, abandoned building, two homeless beggars went to look for shelter one rainy night. Upon finding the building, they quickly went inside due to the heavy rains. They then began searching the place up, finding the scp itself, which surprisingly, was still working. One of them then tried it out. But after playing the game, the scp just turned off. Since then, the beggar is observed being rude, making threats and all that. But the other one dismissed it and slept for the night.
On the following morning, the other beggar was awaken and was invited by unknown people which was turned out to be foundation personnel. As it turns out that the beggar who played the scp was arrested by the police for beating up a rival homeless beggar, who upon interrogation by the police, said some gibberish things, tipping of a foundation agent who was embedded at the station, for the existence of the scp . The agent reported it to his superiors, who in turn authorized an investigation whether there was an anomaly involved in it.
Later testings by d class personnel and analysis would reveal that this was an scp, and that this has to be contained. Later investigation would trace the scp back to a disastrous fire that occurred at a cult's hideout in the 80s
Killing off the entire occupants, destroying everything, except the scp itself.

Hook/Attention-Grabber:
A simple idea of how one thing can change and turn someone, 'evilish'.

Additional Notes:
This is an idea which I salvaged, and revised from it being abandoned a year ago due to personal reasons. I did my best to make this idea into it's best. So feel free to drop any feedback. Suggestions on what to include/remove is appreciated.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: [Seeking Tips and Possibly, Greenlight] an old arcade console

item.title: Birthdays For The Lonely
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes.

Page Type: SCP Article.

Elevator Pitch: The ghost of a 12 year old Caucasian boy who prepares and celebrates birthdays with people following the list of characteristics:

  • absence of both parents/abusive parent(s)
  • low self-esteem
  • not socially active
  • introverted
  • a victim of bullying
  • having little to no friends throughout life
  • no criminal record
  • never celebrated a proper birthday

If said person follows the list, a letter will appear inviting them to a party with directions to an isolated room. Once subject enters the room, in will be a party with a theme based on their interests. They also treat the boy as if he were a childhood friend. At the end of the party, he will give them a present, a physical object of what they wanted since they were younger. If someone interferes or the person ignores the invitation, the party will de-manifest and another apology letter will be sent the next day as well as said gift.

Central Narrative: Invitations were sent to multiple D-Classes in multiple sites the past week. With the foundation made aware of the situation, they decided to test the anomaly. They sent a D-Class to follow the directions which led them to a room where it is currently monitored. There, he celebrates with the SCP and left the room a few hours later with the gift in his hands. Then, they sent another with the purpose of interviewing the SCP. The questions listed are pretty generic, like what their name was, how did they obtain the ability, etc. But there are two questions I would like to highlight:

How he died.
His parents constantly fights which led to his father drinking. Because of that, he would abuse the both of them. On his 12th birthday, he made a wish that him and mother would escape someday. But then, the father came in and killed his son with a bottle before attempting to kill his wife as well before she called the police and he was arrested.

Why he celebrates birthdays.
He wants people to experience what he couldn't and make the world a better place. Also, to make new friends.

After the interview, the D-Class decided to dedicate the party to him and even offered him his own present. In which, the SCP accepts.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: The idea and the narrative tied to it.

Additional Notes: None.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Birthdays For The Lonely

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.content:encoded:
Hi!
RopesAndHopes here! Can call me Ropes or Rops because it's shorter.

I have been a fan of the SCP-verse for SO LONG and I decided that I want to contribute by reading, voting and commenting on articles. Not sure if I want to write articles for now, so I will stick to reading :)

For the random question of the month: best food to eat with friends is definitely pizza. I am probably biased as whenever my friends and I gather for socialising nights we get pizza.


Forum category: Site Announcements and Proposals / Introductions
Forum thread: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers

Ah beans, I did just write about something that can be locked up and forgotten about. Welp, back to the drawing board!
Thanks for your response!


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5127

"and both subjects were offered positions in Foundation security after showing proficiency in responding to anomalous events and maintaining rational thought during a crisis."

Got my +1 in this moment. Love to see a caring foundation :)


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5266

item.title: Re: (seeking greenlight/feedback) a mysterious entity
item.content:encoded:
So, I also like writing mysterious things, but there's a tradeoff in SCP writing between mystery and storytelling: too much mystery and there's not enough story for people to care about. Mystery is only interesting if you've provided enough information that people will fill in the gaps that you left and get an interesting answer. (Which you implied, e.g, you need to have some idea wear that answer is when writing.)

I'm not really getting anything here beyond "ooooo, it's mysterious", and there's definitely not enough to make me care about filling in the gaps.

So, if you've got an idea for what the deal with THEM and the future is, go ahead and spell it out for us, and we can give you feedback on the idea and tell you how much you can afford to hide. If you don't have much of an idea there, then you really need to expand on that and come back, because without it this is almost nothing.

(The entity having a humorous personality isn't bad, but I can hardly say, "yeah, that'll be a great article" just from you saying that.)


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: (seeking greenlight/feedback) a mysterious entity

item.title: The Perfect Sleeper
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre: Drama

Page Layout: A series of incident and experiment logs.

Elevator Pitch: Through a series of accidental occurrences, mental and environmental manipulation the foundation slowly discovers the horrifying and for some readers humorous extent a human individual will go through in order to reach his desired target, a suitable bed. Definition of suitable is affected by many factors and perceptions.

Central Narrative: Incident logs detail a series of increasingly frustrating disappearances for seemingly innocuous reasons that tie into a series of experiments of increasing complexity and difficulty/danger. Including discovery of suspect as result of jail cells bring unable to hold a man convicted of a massive series of breaking and entering.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: Starting with the discovery of the test subject and first incident logs it's immediately apparent that a perfect Houdini has been combined with massive property damage. No lock to complex no wall too strong no bridge to far to stop this man from sleeping. Attempts are made to use this power for unstoppable infiltration.

Additional Notes: While leaving room for imagination it's hinted the subject is suffering from the results of a "Monkeys Paw" wish that included a specified sleeping time. Exact wording is never discovered but it's hinted at including that the subject may be much older than he looks.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: The Perfect Sleeper

item.title: Rose-coloured glasses SCP
item.content:encoded:
no drafts in the forum please


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Rose-coloured glasses SCP

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hello hello Gruvint8! Welcome to the Wiki! I'm Ralliston, a site author and a member of the Community Outreach Team - with this helpful little comment, I'll try to help you find your way around the site and link a bunch of useful places and links that will help you in your SCP journey.

It's awesome to see someone with prior writing experience joining the wiki - I'm sure what you'll come up with during your stay here will be absolutely amazing. I can't wait to see your stuff. It's also great to see that after all of these years "lurking", you finally decided to make an account on-site. You'd be surprised at how many people stay only as lurkers and never properly become members. After all, now you can actively affect the quality of articles posted on-site by voting, you can comment, and, most importantly it seems, contribute.

Without further ado though, let's click this collapsible, shall we? :)

+ show block
– hide block

If you're going to try your hand at writing, try out our Sandbox. You can make yourself a page here to store your drafts, which are in-works articles. Using it, you can link the drafts to reviewers to get their opinions on them before posting. It really helps everyone out a lot.

For brainstorming concepts, try out the Ideas and Brainstorming forum. You can run your ideas by experienced members of the community if you need help refining and fleshing out your concept. It's a very helpful little place for when you want to start your writing journey, especially when you're not sure if the idea is all that great.

If you have a draft ready, make a thread in the Drafts and Critiques forum. Remember to check the stickied posts in the relevant thread for guidelines on how best to use each forum. You can also try the IRC chat for real-time feedback.

From site usage to writing tips, the Guide Hub contains a wealth of resources geared towards helping members produce successful articles. Within the Guides, you will find useful information written by experienced members of the site for the use of newer ones.

The more time you take to familiarize yourself with the SCP style, the drafting process, and the community, the greater your chances are for success! Remember - there is no rush to writing - take your time! The more time you spend on fleshing your ideas out, the more your chance of success becomes!

As far as reading is concerned, you might want to check out Top Rated New Pages, which will display the most popular recent articles. It's helpful to keep up with what's being received well by the community to implement into your very own writing. Alternatively, you might want to look at the User Curated Lists page, where members of the community list and rank their favorite articles in different genres and themes, showing which attempts at such are successful and what the community likes.

Best of luck with your future contributions to the site! I hope you enjoy your time here as much as we do! If you have any more questions, feel free to message me here on Wikidot or on the IRC chats!


Forum category: Site Announcements and Proposals / Introductions
Forum thread: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers

Deceptively engrossing. Zigs when you expect it to zag. Nicely done.


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5195

The article is ok, though in many aspects could be improved, the foundation takes on a clinical tone in articles and idioms are usually not used in the tone, clinical tone is meant to be specific.

The rosy glasses are interesting but it is kind of like a regular anomalous object, the object just gives a rush of dopamine but it is just a pair of magical glasses that makes the wearer happy and doesn't really warrant an article, just contained on one of the security lockers with the key being held by a researcher and having an entry in the list of anomalous objects

I still believe that there's potential in here though, maybe add to it more? Take it more in a direction of your choosing.


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5127

item.title: Re: The Laughing Tree
item.content:encoded:
Hi RED, Thank you for the review.

Some concept details couldn't be included since I hit the 400-word limit, but many of your points are valid. I'll address them below.

+ show block
– hide block

how exactly does the tree convince you to tie someone up?

It targets socially isolated kids. It frames the victim for theft. And this part was removed, but the tree also trips aggressive kids while they are near this victim. (Also, some members of the camp staff are in on it, as they are former victims)

does the tree itself carve the child's name? or do the children do it?

The tree does it.

how exactly do these victims live on to help the tree? do they still have any free will? what happens when they leave the camp? what exactly happens after ones name is carved?- in the immediate future?

They leave with a warped sense of empathy, and enjoy watching other kids go through the same ordeal. Some of them return as camp staff and help "stoke the fire" by laughing at the victim when he asks for help or by putting a stolen item back when the victim tries to get rid of it.

why the child didn't want to go to camp

Prior to camp, he was already socially isolated among peers.

what exactly happened after he got tied to the tree.

The interviewed child is not tied to the tree. He manages to stop its influence by wetting himself. One particular goal of this piece is to show that those that escape still feel shame and have a normal sense of empathy. The interviewed camp staff, however, was tied to the tree in his youth, and now laughs at the experience while disregarding the pain of others.

parts of the narrative that show the parent stepped in?

The parent is not present at the camp. He only steps in during the interview, when his child becomes distraught or to answer the few questions he can.

Regarding your point about how the tree is called to the Foundation's attention. It's true that this is a little lacking. But I think that the foundation would be interested in rumors/tales that allude to a possible SCP entity. Perhaps the investigation department checks the background for names written on the tree and finds a pattern of anti-social behavior among them. Is there something in SCP that could possible help with this point? like a signature for a nefarious group or individual?


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: The Laughing Tree

What do I see in this object? An anomaly and a fairly predictable joke. Yes, the joke is not bad and the design is chosen perfectly, but it is still just a joke and there is nothing behind it. I agree with some of the commenters above, it could very easily be a -J, but this is in the main series.
If you compare this to the very similar in my opinion SCP-3078, you may understand the reason for my disappointment.


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5922

item.title: Re: [Seeking Greenlight] Sinister Fedora.
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the feedback. And no, he doesn't look like The Mask at all. The trickster isn't actually acting in ways like a clown or a cartoon character. The trickster I'm describing isn't also chaotic but enjoys joking and helping others at the same time shown to be always carefree, immature, goofy, unpredictable, and comes off as a slight irresponsible idiot. But sometimes, the character can be serious in some situations. And I didn't say he's energetic nor burst into happiness. He's just enjoying his days like a regular person with nothing to do except for good old fashion walking, hanging out.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: [Seeking Greenlight] Sinister Fedora.

item.title: Re: Chearleader Pom Poms[Seeking Greenlight]
item.content:encoded:
Thanks by the way in the additional notes it says the effects happen till there are no more memories to be erased even if you take the pom poms off. thanks for the nonofficial greenlight now I can go to the nonofficial draft section


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Chearleader Pom Poms[Seeking Greenlight]

item.title: (seeking greenlight/feedback) a mysterious entity
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre: Mystery

Elevator Pitch: A mysterious female being with some anomalys about her like an undefinable age and a bad forecast for the future of humankind.

Central Narrative: The main database entry acts as a brief introduction and explanation to the viewer, the main narrative really happens in the interviews with the SCP. The main point of the story is the entity's forecast of THEM, which are never rally explained and leave room for interpretation.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: The entitys character. Especially in the interviews, the entity seems humorous and lively.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: (seeking greenlight/feedback) a mysterious entity
